on

Tobi Amusan is through to the semi-finals of women’s 100m hurdles at the 2024 Olympic Games.

The world record holder won her heat in a time of 12.49s.

Alaysha Johnson was second in a time of 12.61s, while Janeek Brown came third in 12.84s.

Amusan is one of the favourites to win gold medal in the event.

The 27-year-old finished fourth in the women’s 100m hurdles three years ago in Tokyo.

The semi-final will hold at the Stade de France on Thursday.
